meta.name: PTQ Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire
meta.description: The PTQ is a 15-item self-report scale that assesses an individual’s tendency toward repetitive negative thinking, including rumination and worry. It is a transdiagnostic measure not limited to any specific psychological disorder.
meta.author: Ehring, Zetsche, Weidacker, Wahl, Schönfeld & Ehlers (2011)
meta.reference: Ehring T, Zetsche U, Weidacker K, Wahl K, Schönfeld S, Ehlers A. The Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ): validation of a content-independent measure of repetitive negative thinking. J Behav Ther Exp Psychiatry. 2011;42(2):225-232.
meta.instruction: Think about how you typically think when you experience negative events or problems.

questions.text: The same thoughts keep going through my mind again and again
I think about my problems over and over without finding a solution
Thoughts intrude into my mind related to unpleasant experiences
I can’t stop dwelling on my experiences
I repeatedly think about things that make me unhappy
I keep asking myself questions without finding an answer
My mind is always occupied with my problems
I keep thinking about my failures, shortcomings, and mistakes
I keep thinking about negative things endlessly
My thoughts prevent me from focusing on other things
These thoughts interrupt what I am doing
I can’t stop myself from thinking about them
I keep thinking about what would have happened if I had done something differently
Thinking about these problems or experiences causes me distress
These thoughts make me feel exhausted and worn out

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your level of perseverative negative thinking is low. You can recover well from negative events.
suggestions: Keep up mindfulness practices to sustain psychological resilience

ranges.label: Mild Rumination
ranges.description: You have some degree of repetitive negative thinking and may occasionally get caught in cycles of overthinking.
suggestions: Learn mindfulness meditation to reduce ruminative thinking
Try shifting your attention to positive activities

ranges.label: Moderate Rumination
ranges.description: Your perseverative negative thinking level is elevated. This may affect your daily functioning and emotional well-being.
suggestions: Consider learning cognitive-behavioral techniques to break the rumination cycle
Share your concerns with someone you trust

ranges.label: Severe Rumination
ranges.description: Your perseverative negative thinking level is very high. This may significantly affect your mental health and daily life.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional psychological help
Cognitive-behavioral therapy is very effective in reducing ruminative thinking
Avoid spending time alone overthinking; engage more with others
Monitor for co-occurring depression or anxiety symptoms
